title: Phenomenon 57
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Phenomenon 57: Achronism / Chronophobia; or, Per Inane Peruersum Tempus}}__NOTOC__ {{Background|file=void.png}}__NOTOC__ {{CSS|content= .theme-fandomdesktop-dark { --theme-page-background-color: rgba(0, 0, 0,0.999999999); --theme-page-background-color--secondary: rgba(0, 0, 0,0.9999999999); --theme-page-text-color: #FFFFFF; --theme-border-color: #FFFFFF; --theme-page-dynamic-color-1: #ffffff; --theme-page-dynamic-color-1--rgb: 255,255,255; --theme-sticky-nav-background-color: #000000; --theme-community-header-color: #FFFFFF; } .theme-fandomdesktop-light { --theme-page-background-color: rgba(10, 10, 10,0.999999999); --theme-page-background-color--secondary: rgba(10, 10, 10,0.9999999999); --theme-page-text-color: #FFFFFF; --theme-border-color: #FFFFFF; --theme-page-dynamic-color-1: #ffffff; --theme-page-dynamic-color-1--rgb: 255,255,255; --theme-sticky-nav-background-color: #000000; --theme-community-header-color: #FFFFFF; } li{ --theme-page-text-color: #FFFFFF; } }} {{CSS|content= .theme-fandomdesktop-light { --theme-link-color: #808080 } .theme-fandomdesktop-dark { --theme-link-color: #808080; } }} [[File:WHAS.png|600px|center|link=]] {{Phenomenon Class|frequency=5|effectiveness=3|danger=3}} ==Survival Guide== * Keep a watch in your possession at all times, and make a habit of checking it at regular intervals. * Do not put off sleep, even if you do not currently feel tired. Staying energized and alert is essential. * Some inconsistencies in documentation, dates, and events may be a result of this phenomenon. These are not errors in documentation; this is simply how reality works here. * In more [[Phenomenon 11|unstable]] areas, memories may not align with what events actually happened. Do not simply dismiss these occurences, they are likely not results of mere misjudgement. * If you begin to feel significant deja vu, leave the area immediately. This is not your first time here. ==Overview== {{Blurimage|file=temporal.png|caption=A variety of possible states of [[Level 0]], forced into our singular timeline via a temporal fluctuation.}} As most who remain in this hell for long enough may know, time works radically differently in the Backrooms compared to baseline reality. Phenomenon 57, known collectively as Achronism, is a term used to describe these occurrences: innumerable temporal anomalies which exist within the Backrooms. Distressingly common throughout the Backrooms, this anomaly presents various challenges for those traversing this dimension, with a good grip on the realmвҖҷs chronology being practically required for long-term survival. ==Discovery & History== Due to its nature and effects on the Backrooms as a whole, a definite discovery date for Achronism cannot be ascertained; however, a condensed history of its study can adequately be provided. Throughout all of human history, people have sought to study and explain the mechanisms behind Achronism; common mythologies regarding the phenomenon claimed that it occurred because the Backrooms were a dimension outside of time itself (which is only partially true), or attributed its related anomalies to deific beings. Much later onwards, with the introduction of modern scientific groups such as [[The Kauer Research Organization]] and Brutus Experimentation Organization, more detailed study would begin on Achronism as a phenomenon, allowing for the creation of the modern Temporal Interlacing theory, and by consequence, an explanation for Phenomenon 57. ==Causes== The '''Temporal Interlacing''' theory, created in 1968, posits that the BackroomsвҖҷ temporal makeup consists of a singular timeline, constantly having its different time periods intertwined with and connecting to each other. Due to the singular and self-consistent nature of the timeline, no paradoxes or timeline splitting occurs: once someone enters an earlier or later time period, they were essentially always there from the beginning, with any attempts to change past events resulting in a series of coincidences which prevent the alteration from happening. In effect, this renders tracking causality within the Backrooms unreliable at best and a hopeless ordeal at worst, forcing humans within the Wake to develop new ways to comprehend the passage of time; one of these methods includes the '''Frontrooms Date (FD)''' system. The Frontrooms Date system hinges on the fact that although temporal progression in the Backrooms is unpredictable and chaotic, the progression of the [[The Frontrooms|FrontroomsвҖҷ]] time relative to the Backrooms is entirely linear; thus, someone who no-clips into the Backrooms from 1999 will always appear within the dimension before someone who no-clips in 2000. By interviewing various people who fall into the Backrooms on what date it was when they no-clipped, a rough estimate of the current date can be made, allowing for efficient historical timekeeping. However, this method does have some flaws; gathering information about the current date with this method is inefficient, thus making this essentially useless when measuring shorter timescales. Luckily, another system was developed for this method: during the boom in [[Phenomenon 2|BACK-NET]] site creation during the 1980s, the Wake Historical and Archaeological Society would develop the Andov-Emil Automated Frontrooms Date system. This system, running the progression of time in a certain spot in [[Level 1]], submitted reports by the Wake Historical and Archaeological Society of when newcomers to the Backrooms entered the space, and recovered dates from the occasional Frontrooms website able to be accessed through BACK-NET into an algorithm to make an estimate of the current date in the Frontrooms. ==Related Phenomena== ===Mental Achronism=== The most common result of Achronism is the destabilization of oneвҖҷs Temporal Response Windows (TRWs) and circadian rhythm when entering the Backrooms. Due to small microscopic fluctuations in time, oneвҖҷs mental perception of time is slowly degraded to the point of functional uselessness. To a human mind in this dimension, time lengths numbering in decades can seem to go by in a flash, while mere minutes can feel unbearably long. It is not uncommon for one to perceive a given stretch of time as both extremely long and blindingly short simultaneously, or experience past memories as if they were actively happening to oneself. The consequences of this anomaly are varied: one may remain awake for much longer than is healthy, their circadian rhythm failing to know when to actually fall asleep, one may spend much more time doing something than initially expected, taking up valuable time for other essential tasks, and finally, the diminished comprehension of linear time often causes distress, leaving one vulnerable to ailments such as [[Phenomenon 35|Roth Syndrome]]. ===Time Dilation=== The second most common manifestation of '''Achronism''', namely, Time Dilation, is similar to its counterpart in normal reality. In essence, different sections of a given level will often vary in time speeds, usually in intervals of around 0.5x to 1.5x the level's standard speed of time. This effect is exacerbated when dealing with cross-level time; in a given level, the relative time speed of adjacent levels usually range from -1.0x to 2.0x. Generally, relative time speeds between two levels will differ more the farther the two levels are apart: levels around five levels apart will often differ in time scales from around -3.0x to 6.0x the speed of one's starting level. Although this property makes timekeeping difficult, there is one exception to this anomaly that has provided a saving grace: the clock. All standard analog clocks or watches, whether they be from the normal reality or the Backrooms, will be unaffected by Time Dilation, remaining at the same relative speed of time as that of the Frontrooms (if it originates there) or its parent level. These can by extent be used to both perform effective timekeeping and track the time dilation of a certain area. ===Temporal Gateways=== '''Temporal Gateways''', also known as "Time Rifts", are fixed spatial points within the Backrooms which allow for access to a specific point in time, whether it be in the future or the past. Common within [[:Category:Glitched Levels|more unstable levels]], these points are often accompanied by an increased frequency of other subtypes of Temporal Distortion within the area, often to a concerning degree. Although these thresholds might be initially unassuming, the potential risk of crossing through one is hard to overstate. Due to the near instant anomalous temporal travel these anomalies provide, the environment one travels through may be entirely different on the other side, making most prior information one has collected unreliable at best. ===Chronological Loops=== '''Chronological Loops''' are essentially what they say on the tin: periodic resets in time until the loop either ends or the person(s) is removed from the time-looped area. Without outside interference, events usually transpire similarly with every loop, save for a reported feeling of deja vu. The most famous instance of this is the '''Battle of the Field''', a battle between 400 [[M.E.G]] and [[Republic of Aurielle|Auriellen]] soldier which started in 2005, and has been repeating in a time loop ever since. Attempts to rescue the soldiers have been deemed unnecessary: as those who were not originally in the looped area when a time loop starts are rendered irrecoverable upon its reset, attempting to do so is dangerous at best. ===Temporal Disturbances=== When creating theories about the nature of Achronism, scientists initially assumed that our universe was a deterministic one due to the inability to change events via traveling back in time: in reality, this is only partially true. '''Temporal Disturbances''' are the most mysterious sub-phenomenon of Achronism, occurring as a result of the natural [[Phenomenon 11|instability]] of the Backrooms. In essence, although there is only one timeline per dimension in the manifold of [[Uncharted Territory]], the current timeline is constantly changing, with the intensity of local timeline alteration depending on the instability of a given area. The effects of Temporal Disturbances vary, but commonly include retroactive alterations to history, events which do not correctly line up, and in r re ca s, the temp ra y d s uti on of tr i o al c li

Year XXXX
XX:XX
Tick. Tock. Tick. Tock. Tick. Tock. The clock kept its rhythm through the vast [[Level 1|parking lot]] the man was in, mocking him with every movement, its perfect timekeeping his mental undoing. Already, his mind had began to wither from its display. How long had it been since he had arrived here? Days? Years? Decades? Centuries? Millenia? Eons? No. No time. No time at all. There was no time anymore. Not here, at least. The last time he had been in the Frontrooms was the last time he had been a part of time, a part of comprehension, a part of reason. When he had fallen from the heaven, all of that was ripped away, replaced by an ever-constant blur of confusion and terror. A puzzle without a solution, a maze without an exit, suffering without a resolution. The man's grip on time had softened, and finally he began to remember and forget at once. He was six, celebrating his birthday with a few other children whose names had long since been lost to time. He was sixteen, covered in blood and standing over the body of the man who his car had crushed. He was twenty, enduring the harsh lights of the prison building. He was twenty-three, fighting in a war against the [[M.E.G]]. He was thirty, starving in the darkness as the [[Phenomenon 12|lights flickered out]]. He was everywhere, every when, always, never. His perception of time quickened, the clock's hands spinning ever faster and his body decaying from hunger along with it. Already he began to hear death calling, his fate approaching and receding in front of him. The man didn't care anymore. Our personhood had been stripped from us the moment we entered this purgatory. We do not live, we simply survive, cursed to travel towards a nonexistent horizon. And such the liminality of this dimension is bestowed on us as a whole: no past, no future. Only an infinite present.
